Description: An air compressor system (coupled with a refrigerated air dryer) is necessary to generate high-pressure air to deliver to a Peak Infinity 5060 nitrogen generator to be purified into 99.95% pure nitrogen gas. Title: BQM-177A Subsonic Aerial Target System Evaluation and Repair Services
Awardee: KRATOS UNMANNED AERIAL SYSTEMS, INC Sacramento CA 95838 USA
Description: This is an Award Notice prepared in accordance with FAR Part 15 Contracting by Negotiation procedures. Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) awarded contract N0001926D0006 to Kratos Unmanned Aerial Systems, Inc. (KUAS), 5381 Raley Boulevard, Sacramento, CA 95838. KUAS will provide test, teardown, and evaluation (TTE) and repair services in support of the BQM-177A Subsonic Aerial Target System, a subsonic recoverable, aerial target system used in weapon systems test and evaluation and Fleet training. This effort was issued as a sole source to KUAS under NAICS Code 336414 (Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing) as well as the Product Service Code (PSC) J014 (Maintenance, Repair, and Rebuilding of Equipment- Guided Missiles) which resulted in the placement of a Single Award Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract with five (5) one (1) year ordering periods. The contract consists of cost-plus-fixed-fee (CPFF) CLINs for TTE and Repairs and cost-reimbursable CLINs for Travel. This synopsis is for informational purposes only.

Title: 15B11826Q000000004 3RD QTR FY 26 FOOD SERVICE BREAD USP BIG SANDY Award Notice
Sub-Tier: FEDERAL PRISON SYSTEM / BUREAU OF PRISONS
Awardee: East baking Co Inez KY 41224 USA
Description: U.S. Department of Justice Federal Bureau of Prisons USP Big Sandy 1197 Airport Road Inez, KY 41224 March 20, 2026 MEMORANDUM FOR PROCUREMENT FILE LOCATION: USP BIG SANDY SUBJECT: Award Decision/Determination of Responsibility Memo Fiscal Year 26, Quarter 3 Bread Subsistence Requirement RFQ#: 15B11825Q000000004 BPQ#: VARIOUS/SEE FILE Purchase Order#: VARIOUS/SEE FILE Request for Contract Action BPQ was received by USP BIG SANDY by contract staff on 03/01/2026. This was accepted by Contract Specialist, Jacob Flanigan, on 03/01/2026. As stated in the Request for Quote, “Award will be made to the responsible Quoter whose quote conforms to the requirements and is determined to provide the best value to the Government, considering the evaluation factors outlined below: Price - The government will evaluate the Quoter’s proposed price to ensure it is fair and reasonable; (2) Past Performance - Past performance information shall include prior experience and similar performances undertaken by the contractor;” ALL VENDORS were verified to be a small business in accordance with NAICS 311812 - Commercial Bakeries small business size of 1000 employees. ALL VENDORS also has a good past performance with no recent Termination for Causes. See file for www.SAM.gov responsibilities and determinations. With the final pricing of all contractors, the below abstract represents the best value considering price, see abstract below. No. Item Description Qty Unit LOWEST PRICE LOWEST VENDOR 1 Bread, Roll, Enriched, Finger (Hot Dog) 2890 PG 2.2600 East Baking 2 Bread, Roll, Enriched, Sandwich (Hamburger) 4331 PG 2.2600 East Baking 3 Bread, White, Whole Wheat 12240 PG 2.2600 East Baking Therefore, each vendor is the best value, per line item, and will win the award above. Per FAR 9.103, (a) Purchases shall be made from, and contracts shall be awarded to, responsible prospective contractors only. (b) No purchase or award shall be made unless the contracting officer makes an affirmative determination of responsibility. I believe each vendor is responsible. This memo will serve as a determination of responsibility. All information within this memo is complete and to the best of my knowledge. Jacob Flanigan Contract Specialist USP Big Sandy Federal Bureau of Prisons

Title: COLUMBIA-Class Ballistic Missile Submarine Design, Class Lead Yard Support and Sustainment, Integrated Enterprise Plan Initiatives for the Class, and Submarine Industrial Base Supplier Development Efforts
Awardee: ELECTRIC BOAT CORPORATION Groton CT 06340 USA
Description: The contract action was not awarded competitively and was not fixed price, in accordance with FAR 6.302-1(a)(2)(iii) - only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements. Due to the federal government shutdown in October-November 2025 and pursuant to DFARS 252.232-7998 Obligations in Advance of Fiscal Year 2026 Funding (DEVIATION 2026-O0001), an initial cost-only modification was awarded in the amount of $2,283,291,317 in November 2025. Once FY26 funding became available in March 2026, a larger modification was awarded manifesting the $2,283,291,317 along with an additional $15,383,494,792 for a combined total of $17,666,786,109. General Dynamics Electric Boat Corp., Groton, Connecticut, was awarded a $2,283,291,317 cost-only modification to previously awarded contract N00024-17-C-2117 for additional Advance Procurement / Advance Construction for SSBN 828 through SSBN 832 and to support additional COLUMBIA Ballistic Missile Submarines (SSBNs) program execution in accordance with DFARS 252.232-7998 Obligations in Advance of Fiscal Year 2026 Funding (DEVIATION 2026-O0001). Work will be performed in Groton, Connecticut (70%); Newport News, Virginia (15%); and Quonset Point, Rhode Island (15%), and is expected to be completed by December 2031. No funding was obligated at the time of award. Pursuant to DFARS 252.232-7998 Obligations in Advance of Fiscal Year 2026 Funding (DEVIATION 2026-O0001), Fiscal 2026 National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund funds in the amount of $2,229,067,000 (98%) and Fiscal 2026 other procurement (Navy) funds in the amount of $54,224,317 (2%) to be obligated at the time made available and will not expire at the end of the current fiscal year. General Dynamics Electric Boat Corp., Groton, Connecticut, was awarded a $15,383,494,792 cost-plus-incentive-fee and cost-plus-fixed-fee modification to a previously awarded contract (N00024-17-C-2117) for additional Columbia-class ballistic missile submarines design, class lead yard support and sustainment, integrated enterprise plan initiatives for the class, and submarine industrial base supplier development enhancement efforts to support Columbia-class ballistic missile submarine program execution. The industrial base development work is for the furtherance of the Navy’s plan of serial production of Columbia- and Virginia-class attack submarines. Funding is pursuant to the Consolidated Appropriations Act 2026 (Public Law 119-75). Work will be performed in Groton, Connecticut (21%); Newport News, Virginia (6%); Sunnyvale, California (5%); Menomonee, Wisconsin (4%); Quonset Point, Rhode Island (4%); Fitchburg, Massachusetts (3%); Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (3%); Mobile, Alabama (2%); Bethlehem, Pennsylvania (1%); and other locations less than 1% (51%), and is expected to be completed by June 2035. Fiscal 2026 national sea-based deterrence fund funds in the amount of $3,068,073,260 (95%); fiscal 2025 maritime industrial base funds in the amount of $74,000,000 (2%); fiscal 2025 research, development, test and evaluation (Navy) funds in the amount of $73,214,000 (2%); and fiscal 2026 research, development, test and evaluation (Navy) funds in the amount of $2,837,000 (<1%), will be obligated at the time of award, of which $3,144,910,260 will not expire at the end of the current fiscal year. Naval Sea Systems Command, Washington, D.C., is the contracting activity. NAVSEA contract with General Dynamics Electric Boat Corp. for $15,383,494,792 NAVSEA POC: NAVSEA Office of Corporate Communications, (202) 781-4123 General Dynamics Electric Boat Corp. POC: Myra Lee, Director Communications (860) 433-2086

Title: DSCR Building 31 Roof Repair (A-E Services)
Description: This Justification is being published as required by RFO 6.301(a). Under the authority of RFO 6.103-1, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), awarded a contract action, on a sole source basis, to Woolpert-RS&H LLC (CAGE 7JG89)("Designer of Record, or "DOR"). The contract was awarded in the amount of $632,436.63. This sole-source contract action acquires architect-engineer (A-E) design and construction support services from the DOR, related to the repair of Building 31 Roof, Bays I-M, at Defense Supply Center Richmond (DSCR). The services acquired shall be completed in accordance with all applicable engineering requirements and consistent with the design intent reflected in the construction documents previously prepared by the DOR. The work performed under the contract action constitutes a continuation of services previously performed by the DOR to ensure consistent application of the DOR’s design intent and to maintain DOR responsibility over the construction documents. The redacted Justification and Approval document for this project has been attached. The construction contract for this project will be a separate procurement at a later date.
